section.heading: Crash avoidance technology

subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.chapeau: The Secretary of Transportation shall promulgate a rule—

paragraph.content: to establish minimum performance standards with respect to crash avoidance technology; and

paragraph.chapeau: to require that all passenger motor vehicles manufactured for sale in the United States on or after the compliance date described in subsection (b) shall be equipped with—

subparagraph.chapeau: a forward collision warning and automatic emergency braking system that—

subclause.content: the distance to a vehicle ahead or an object in the path of travel ahead is closing too quickly; and

clause.content: automatically applies the brakes if the driver fails to do so; and

subparagraph.chapeau: a lane departure warning and lane-keeping assist system that—

clause.content: warns the driver to maintain the lane of travel; and
corrects the course of travel if the driver fails to do so.

subsection.heading: Compliance Date.—
subsection.content: The Secretary of Transportation shall determine the appropriate effective date, and any phasing-in of requirements, of the final rule promulgated pursuant to subsection (a).

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 117–58, div. B, title IV, § 24208(a), Nov. 15, 2021, 135 Stat. 823.)
